summary:
The process begins prenatal but significant calcification occurs shortly after birth; however, full development is more closely aligned with when they erupt between ages five and seven for most children. To be precise regarding options given, choice B fits best considering early stages are completed by then yet not all final hardening has occurred upon emergence. Thus, option A or C could also fit depending if partial vs complete calcification was implied respectively though exact timing varies slightly based on individual variation among patients.
The primary focus here should remain consistent across responses – recognizing initial phases commence earlier followed later by visible changes correlating strongly near six-years-old milestone noted clinically consistently throughout pediatric dental care guidelines. Agent1 concludes their response focusing primarily on developmental milestones related directly back towards core query posed initially about onset relative timeframes concerning first permanent molar
